meaning: adj.害羞的,羞怯的,谦逊的,不惹眼的,羞耻的
examples: There was a long silence, and my father looked shamefaced.
In introducing this month's theme, I fell just a little shamefaced.
A boy saw a girl, he liked her, she smiled shamefaced at him, giving him hope.
"You still haven't told us why your salaries are so high, " Luis Villalobos , the group's founder, admonishes a shamefaced man selling spa magazines.
He, at least, had had the grace to be shamefaced.
